subject: HTML, CSS and JavaScript
topics: HTML document structure and semantic markup
Basic text tags — h1 to h6, p, br, hr
Links and images — a, img tags
Semantic elements — header, nav, main, section, article, aside, footer
Collapsible content, figure captions, time elements
Tables — thead, tbody, tfoot, captions, merged cells
Lists — UL, OL, DL and nested lists
Forms — text inputs, radio buttons, checkboxes, select, textarea, datalist
Media elements — video, audio, iframe, embed, canvas, svg

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to define a self-contained composition in a document, like a blog post or news article?
questions.explanation: The `<article>` tag specifically represents a self-contained composition that is independently distributable or reusable.
questions.hint: Think about what constitutes a complete, standalone piece of content.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using semantic HTML elements like `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, etc.?
questions.explanation: Semantic elements provide meaning to the content, which helps search engines and assistive technologies understand the page structure.
questions.hint: Consider how screen readers and search engines interpret HTML.

questions.question: Which attribute is essential for providing alternative text for an image, improving accessibility?
questions.explanation: The `alt` attribute provides a text description of the image, which is read by screen readers and displayed if the image fails to load.
questions.hint: Think about users who cannot see the image.

questions.question: What is the correct way to create a hyperlink to 'https://www.example.com' with the text 'Example Website'?
questions.explanation: The `<a>` tag with the `href` attribute is the standard way to create hyperlinks in HTML.
questions.hint: Remember the basic syntax for creating links.

questions.question: Which tag is used to define a horizontal rule in HTML?
questions.explanation: The `<hr>` tag represents a thematic break between elements, typically displayed as a horizontal rule.
questions.hint: It's a simple tag for visual separation.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<time>` element?
options: To represent a specific date and/or time.
questions.explanation: The `<time>` element represents a specific date and/or time, often used for machine-readable dates.
questions.hint: Consider how you might mark up a publication date.

questions.question: How do you create a nested unordered list in HTML?
questions.explanation: Nested lists are created by placing one list (UL or OL) inside another list item.
questions.hint: Think about how list items can contain other elements.

questions.question: What is the function of the `colspan` attribute in a `<td>` tag?
questions.explanation: The `colspan` attribute specifies how many columns a table cell should span.
questions.hint: It's related to the structure of the table.

questions.question: Which form input type is best suited for allowing users to select a file from their computer?
questions.explanation: The `file` input type allows users to choose a file for upload.
questions.hint: Think about what input is needed for file uploads.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<datalist>` tag?
options: To provide a list of predefined options for an input field.
To store data on the client-side.
questions.explanation: The `<datalist>` tag provides a list of predefined options that can be used with an `<input>` element, offering autocomplete functionality.
questions.hint: It's often used with the `<input>` tag to suggest values.

questions.question: What does the `required` attribute do when added to an input field?
options: It sets a default value for the field.
It forces the user to fill in the field before submitting the form.
questions.explanation: The `required` attribute ensures that the input field must be filled in before the form can be submitted.
questions.hint: Think about form validation.

questions.question: Which tag is used to group related form elements together?
questions.explanation: The `<fieldset>` tag is used to group related elements in a form, often with a `<legend>` to provide a caption.
questions.hint: It helps organize form elements logically.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `muted` attribute on a `<video>` or `<audio>` element?
questions.explanation: The `muted` attribute specifies that the audio should be initially muted.
questions.hint: Consider user experience and autoplay policies.

questions.question: What is the primary use of the `<iframe>` tag?
options: To embed another HTML document within the current page.
To define a section of content.
questions.explanation: The `<iframe>` tag is used to embed another HTML document within the current page, effectively creating an inline frame.
questions.hint: Think about embedding content from other sources.

questions.question: Which element is used to draw graphics on a web page using JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The `<canvas>` element provides a bitmap area that can be dynamically rendered using JavaScript.
questions.hint: It's a blank slate for drawing.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<svg>` element?
questions.explanation: The `<svg>` element is used to define vector-based graphics, which are scalable without losing quality.
questions.hint: Think about images that don't pixelate when zoomed.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to define a navigation menu?
questions.explanation: The `<nav>` tag represents a section of a page that contains navigation links.
questions.hint: It's where you'd put your website's menu.

questions.question: What is the difference between `<div>` and `<section>`?
options: `<div>` is used for inline elements, `<section>` for block elements.
questions.explanation: `<section>` represents a thematic grouping of content, while `<div>` is a generic container with no semantic meaning.
questions.hint: Consider the purpose and meaning of each element.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<caption>` tag in an HTML table?
options: To provide a title or description for the table.
questions.explanation: The `<caption>` tag provides a title or description for the table, helping users understand its content.
questions.hint: It's like a title for the entire table.

questions.question: Which attribute of the `<input>` tag specifies the maximum number of characters allowed in a text field?
questions.explanation: The `maxlength` attribute limits the number of characters a user can enter into a text input field.
questions.hint: It controls the input length.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `loop` attribute in the `<audio>` or `<video>` tag?
options: To make the media play continuously.
questions.explanation: The `loop` attribute causes the media to play repeatedly when it reaches the end.
questions.hint: Think about how to make a video or audio clip repeat.

questions.question:
Consider the following HTML: `

This is bold text and important text.

`. What is the semantic difference between `` and ``?
options: `<b>` makes text bold, `<strong>` makes text italic.
`<strong>` indicates importance, `<b>` simply makes text bold.
questions.explanation: `<strong>` indicates that the text has strong importance, while `<b>` simply makes the text bold without conveying semantic meaning.
questions.hint: Think about the meaning behind the styling.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `type` attribute in a `<button>` tag?
options: To define the button's behavior (submit, reset, button).
questions.explanation: The `type` attribute defines the button's behavior, such as submitting a form, resetting a form, or performing a JavaScript action.
questions.hint: It determines what happens when the button is clicked.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct way to create a radio button group where only one option can be selected?
questions.explanation: Radio buttons with the same `name` attribute create a group where only one option can be selected.
questions.hint: Think about how radio buttons behave in forms.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `id` attribute in HTML?
options: To uniquely identify an element on the page.
questions.explanation: The `id` attribute provides a unique identifier for an element, allowing it to be targeted by CSS, JavaScript, or internal links.
questions.hint: It's used for specific targeting.

questions.question: What is the function of the `<thead>` element within an HTML table?
questions.explanation: The `<thead>` element contains the header rows of a table, typically containing column headings.
questions.hint: It's where you put the column titles.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `value` attribute in an `<input>` tag?
options: To specify the initial value of the input field.
To define the input field's width.
To set the input field's color.
questions.explanation: The `value` attribute sets the initial value of the input field, which can be pre-filled or submitted with the form.
questions.hint: It's the default content of the input.

questions.question: Which tag is used to create a definition list?
questions.explanation: The `<dl>` tag represents a definition list, typically used for displaying a list of terms and their definitions.
questions.hint: Think about lists of terms and their explanations.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `formaction` attribute in an `<input type='submit'>` tag?
options: To specify the URL to which the form data is submitted.
To specify the form's encoding type.
To specify the form's target window.
questions.explanation: The `formaction` attribute allows you to override the form's `action` attribute for a specific submit button.
questions.hint: It's useful for submitting to different URLs based on the button clicked.

questions.question: What is the difference between `colspan` and `rowspan` attributes in a table?
options: `colspan` merges cells horizontally, `rowspan` merges cells vertically.
`colspan` merges cells vertically, `rowspan` merges cells horizontally.
questions.explanation: `colspan` makes a cell span multiple columns, while `rowspan` makes a cell span multiple rows.
questions.hint: Think about the direction of the merging.

questions.question: You want to create a collapsible section. Which HTML elements would be most appropriate to achieve this without JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The `<details>` element creates a disclosure widget, and the `<summary>` element provides a visible heading for the collapsible content.
questions.hint: Look for elements designed for revealing/hiding content.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `poster` attribute in the `<video>` tag?
options: To display an image before the video starts playing.
questions.explanation: The `poster` attribute specifies an image to be displayed while the video is downloading or before it is played.
questions.hint: It's a placeholder image for the video.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a semantic HTML element?
questions.explanation: The `<div>` element is a generic container and does not have any inherent semantic meaning.
questions.hint: Consider which element describes the *meaning* of the content.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `autocomplete` attribute in an `<input>` tag?
options: To provide suggestions as the user types.
questions.explanation: The `autocomplete` attribute enables or disables autocomplete functionality for the input field.
questions.hint: Think about how browsers suggest values as you type.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using semantic HTML over using only `<div>` elements?
questions.explanation: Semantic HTML provides meaning to the content, which improves SEO, accessibility, and maintainability.
questions.hint: Consider how search engines and assistive technologies interpret HTML.

questions.question: You have a table with complex data. Which attribute would you use to provide a descriptive label for the entire table?
questions.explanation: The `<caption>` tag provides a title or description for the table, making it more accessible and understandable.
questions.hint: It's like a title for the whole table.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `enctype` attribute in the `<form>` tag?
options: To specify the URL to which the form data is submitted.
To specify the encoding type of the form data.
To specify the form's target window.
questions.explanation: The `enctype` attribute specifies how the form data should be encoded when submitting it to the server.
questions.hint: It's related to how the data is transmitted.

questions.question: Which HTML element is used to define a section of a document that is intended to be displayed as a sidebar?
questions.explanation: The `<aside>` element represents a section of a page that is tangentially related to the main content.
questions.hint: Think about content that's related but not central.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `download` attribute in the `<a>` tag?
options: To force the browser to download the linked file.
To open the linked file in a new tab.
To display a download progress bar.
questions.explanation: The `download` attribute indicates that the browser should download the linked file instead of navigating to it.
questions.hint: It's used for providing downloadable files.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<figure>` and `<figcaption>` elements?
options: To encapsulate self-contained content like images and provide a caption.
questions.explanation: The `<figure>` element represents self-contained content, and the `<figcaption>` element provides a caption for that content.
questions.hint: Think about images with descriptive text.

questions.question: What is the difference between `<ol>` and `<ul>`?
options: `<ol>` creates an ordered list, `<ul>` creates an unordered list.
`<ul>` creates an ordered list, `<ol>` creates an unordered list.
`<ol>` is for definitions, `<ul>` is for items.
questions.explanation: `<ol>` creates a numbered list, while `<ul>` creates a bulleted list.
questions.hint: Consider the visual appearance of each list type.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `formmethod` attribute in the `<form>` tag?
options: To specify the HTTP method used to submit the form (GET or POST).
To specify the form's target window.
questions.explanation: The `method` attribute specifies the HTTP method used to submit the form data, typically 'GET' or 'POST'.
questions.hint: It determines how the data is sent to the server.

questions.question: Which attribute is used to specify a keyboard shortcut for an element?
questions.explanation: The `accesskey` attribute assigns a keyboard shortcut to an element, allowing users to access it quickly.
questions.hint: It's used for accessibility and efficiency.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `scope` attribute in a `<th>` (table header) element?
options: To specify the header's text color.
To specify whether the header applies to a row or column.
questions.explanation: The `scope` attribute indicates whether the header applies to a row (`row`) or a column (`col`) of the table.
questions.hint: It helps assistive technologies understand the table structure.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `controls` attribute in the `<video>` or `<audio>` tag?
options: To display playback controls (play, pause, volume).
questions.explanation: The `controls` attribute adds default playback controls to the video or audio element.
questions.hint: It allows the user to interact with the media.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<mark>` element?
options: To define a section of a document.
To highlight a portion of text.
questions.explanation: The `<mark>` element represents text that should be highlighted or marked for reference purposes.
questions.hint: It's used to draw attention to specific text.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `target` attribute in the `<a>` tag?
options: To specify where to open the linked document.
questions.explanation: The `target` attribute specifies where to open the linked document, such as in a new tab (`_blank`) or the same frame (`_self`).
questions.hint: It controls where the link opens.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `placeholder` attribute in an `<input>` tag?
options: To set the input's default value.
To display a hint inside the input field before the user enters text.
questions.explanation: The `placeholder` attribute displays a hint inside the input field that disappears when the user starts typing.
questions.hint: It's a helpful guide for the user.

questions.question: Which attribute is used to specify a unique name for a form element?
questions.explanation: The `name` attribute is used to identify form elements when submitting the form data to the server.
questions.hint: It's how the server recognizes the input.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `<progress>` element?
questions.explanation: The `<progress>` element represents the progress of a task, typically displayed as a progress bar.
questions.hint: It's used to show how much of a task is complete.
